name: Assam Spicy Shrimp
recipeInstructions:
1. Break the tamarind pulp apart and place it in a small saucepan with water. Simmer, covered, for about 10 minutes or until a paste separates from the seeds. Press through a sieve or food mill to extract all pulp and soupy liquid. Discard the seeds.
2. Rinse shrimp thoroughly and pat dry. Marinate for 1 hour with 1 tablespoon of this tamarind liquid, oyster sauce, and hot chili sauce.
3. Cut young ginger into small slivers. If using mature ginger, peel and slice it.
4. Peel and slice garlic.
5. In a non-stick pan, gently heat blachan for 3 to 4 minutes to develop flavors.
6. Peel and cut red onions into wedges.
7. Combine oyster sauce, dark soy sauce, and hot chili sauce with the blachan in a bowl.
8. Heat oil in a large wok over high heat until it sizzles but does not smoke.
9. Add ginger and garlic to the wok and stir-fry for about 3 to 4 minutes until golden brown.
10. Add shrimp in batches and stir-fry until they are orange-red, about 3 minutes.
11. Pour the sauce mixture over the shrimp and continue cooking at a brisk boil until aromatic and sauces have reduced and thickened, enrobing the shells, about 8 minutes.
12. Mix in brown sugar and onion wedges. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed for sweetness and spiciness.
13. Serves 6 (as a main dish with rice) or 8 (with other dishes).
